Class EdnaMap<K,V>

java.lang.Object
java.util.AbstractMap<K,V>
de.kleinert.edna.data.EdnaMap<K,V>
Type Parameters:
K -
V -
All Implemented Interfaces:
IObj, Map<K,V>, SequencedMap<K,V>
@Unmodifiable public final class EdnaMap<K,V> extends AbstractMap<K,V> implements SequencedMap<K,V>, IObj
All collection types in Edna are unmodifiable, meaning that all mutating methods throw exceptions.

  • Nested Class Summary

    Nested classes/interfaces inherited from class AbstractMap

    AbstractMap.SimpleEntry<K,V>, AbstractMap.SimpleImmutableEntry<K,V>

    Nested classes/interfaces inherited from interface Map

    Map.Entry<K,V>

  • Method Details

    • of

      @NotNull public static <K,V> @NotNull EdnaMap<K,V> of(K k, V v)
      Create a new instance with the provided keys and values.
      Type Parameters:
      K - Key type.
      V - Value type.
      Returns:
      A new instance.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if there are duplicate keys or if there is a key without an associated value.
      See Also:
      • create(List)

    • of

      @NotNull public static <K,V> @NotNull EdnaMap<K,V> of(K k1, V v1, K k2, V v2)

      Type Parameters:
      K - Key type.
      V - Value type.
      Parameters:
      k1 - Key 1.
      v1 - Value 1.
      k2 - Key 2.
      v2 - Value 2.
      Returns:
      A new instance.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if there are duplicate keys or if there is a key without an associated value.

    • of

      @NotNull public static <K,V> @NotNull EdnaMap<K,V> of(Object... kvs)
      Create a new instance with the provided keys and values. It is assumed that the argument array consists of keys and values of the specified types.
      Type Parameters:
      K - Key type.
      V - Value type.
      Parameters:
      kvs - Keys and values as a variadic argument.
      Returns:
      A new instance.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if there are duplicate keys or if there is a key without an associated value.
      See Also:
      • create(List)

    • create

      @NotNull public static <K,V> @NotNull EdnaMap<K,V> create(@NotNull @NotNull List<Object> kvs)
      Create a new instance with the provided keys and values. 
          
    EdnaMap.create(List.of(1, "a", 2, "b", 3, "c", 4, "d", 5, "e", 6, "f", 7, "g"))
    // => {1=a, 2=b, 3=c, 4=d, 5=e, 6=f, 7=g} (but as Map<Object, Object>

    EdnaMap.<Integer, String>create(List.of(1, "a", 2, "b", 3, "c", 4, "d", 5, "e", 6, "f", 7, "g"))
    // => {1=a, 2=b, 3=c, 4=d, 5=e, 6=f, 7=g}
      Type Parameters:
      K - Key type.
      V - Value type.
      Parameters:
      kvs - Keys and values as a list.
      Returns:
      A new instance.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if there are duplicate keys or if there is a key without an associated value.

    • createFromEntries

      @NotNull public static <K,V> @NotNull EdnaMap<K,V> createFromEntries(@NotNull @NotNull List<Map.Entry<K,V>> kvs)
      Create a new instance with the provided entries. 
          
    EdnaMap.create(List.of(1, "a", 2, "b", 3, "c", 4, "d", 5, "e", 6, "f", 7, "g"))
    // => {1=a, 2=b, 3=c, 4=d, 5=e, 6=f, 7=g} (but as Map<Object, Object>

    EdnaMap.<Integer, String>create(List.of(1, "a", 2, "b", 3, "c", 4, "d", 5, "e", 6, "f", 7, "g"))
    // => {1=a, 2=b, 3=c, 4=d, 5=e, 6=f, 7=g}
      Type Parameters:
      K - Key type.
      V - Value type.
      Parameters:
      kvs - Keys and values as a list.
      Returns:
      A new instance.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if there are duplicate keys or if there is a key without an associated value.

    • create

      @NotNull public static <K,V> @NotNull EdnaMap<K,V> create(@NotNull @NotNull Map<K,V> kvs)
      Create a new instance with the provided Map. If the input is a SequencedMap, the implementation guarantees that the order of elements is preserved when copying.
      Type Parameters:
      K - Key type.
      V - Value type.
      Parameters:
      kvs - The Map.
      Returns:
      A new instanced.

      @NotNull public @Unmodifiable @NotNull Map<Object,Object> meta()
      Description copied from interface: IObj
      Returns the metadata of the object. The metadata is an immutable Map.

      the metadata.
